body.title: Granny bug: skip tickets already closed/done before escalating
body.result: persist alerted_ids to disk; dedup channel posts in _hold_for_cc_approval and _hold_for_audit_fail; 5 new tests
body.description:
Granny re-escalated T-sec-killswitch-authz, T-announce-proof-validation, T-sec-shim-coldstart-failclosed, T-provenance-write-enforce every ~60 seconds from 03:41–03:44 AM on 2026-06-02. All four tickets were already closed. Root cause: _alerted_ids is in-memory; after a Granny restart it's empty, so closed tickets that were previously alerted get re-escalated on the next poll.

Fix: in run_once(), before escalating a ticket, re-check its status from queue. If status is closed/done/cancelled, add to _alerted_ids and skip silently. Persist _alerted_ids alongside _dispatched_ids (already external-state pattern from T-granny-external-state).

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (run_once escalation path), devices/granny/orchestrator.py if relevant
**Design rules:** external state principle — _alerted_ids must survive restart
**Scope boundary:** escalation dedup only; does not change when Granny escalates (HIGH-inertia logic unchanged), only prevents re-escalation of already-closed tickets
**Completion criteria:** after Granny restart, closed tickets that were previously escalated do not re-appear in channel; _alerted_ids persisted to disk; test: restart Granny with a pre-closed escalated ticket in alerted state — no channel post on next run_once()
